title: stylidium aeonioides
intro: Stylidium aeonioides, also known as the dropper or trigger plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. This plant is native to Western Australia and is known for its unique, drooping leaves that resemble a trigger. The medicinal properties of Stylidium aeonioides include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and promoting digestion.
